title: Pseudobaeckea villosa
intro: Pseudobaeckea villosa, also known as the hairy Pseudobaeckea, is a shrub-like plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Bruniaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including treating fever, coughs, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Pseudobaeckea villosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its potential health benefits, such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, often used to help alleviate respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
